userAuthoredOraclePolicyText:
For every requirement class, expected values must originate from the strongest binding-required oracle that is executable, mechanically extractable, or otherwise independently observable.

An executable reference adapter is mandatory whenever the governing authority or a faithful reference implementation can reasonably be built, run, instrumented, or observed with available tools.

Implementation cost, inconvenience, task size, absence of an existing adapter, or producer preference does not make an oracle unavailable.

`hand-derived-exact` is forbidden unless I separately authorize it for a specifically named requirement class after retained evidence demonstrates that every stronger required oracle is unavailable.

Authority priority and oracle availability are different:

* Stronger authority determines the behavior that PHOSPHENE must reproduce.
* A lower-tier executable implementation may provide measurements but may not silently override stronger authority.
* Any disagreement must produce an explicit authority-conflict record and remain FAIL until resolved.

Preserve this section verbatim as the user-authored source for the binding's oracle-precedence policy.

requirementClasses.description: MilkDrop per-frame EEL expression evaluation over explicit variable pools — operator subset first, function library and uninitialized-read semantics as subsequent claims. Listed before requirement-class.milkdrop-behavior because pattern matching selects the first matching class.

semanticAcceptanceContracts.operativeMeaning: Every mandatory evidence class has a retained artifact that the claim verification chain actually consumes
semanticAcceptanceContracts.mechanicalAcceptanceCondition: engine + bindingfields readers named in the framework matrix row for this contract; see verify framework bindingFieldAudit

semanticAcceptanceContracts.operativeMeaning: Every mandatory positive control resolves to an executable check registered on every class claim whose own retained execution result passes
semanticAcceptanceContracts.mechanicalAcceptanceCondition: engine + bindingfields readers named in the framework matrix row for this contract; see verify framework bindingFieldAudit

semanticAcceptanceContracts.operativeMeaning: Every mandatory negative-control defect class has a registered evaluator exercised by class fixtures and rejected with its intended signature
